title: King Arthur and The Emergence of Confusing Meta Narrative about Paganism and Christianity
content:
**King Arthur** is the ideal king from seemingly unideal upbringing. Born the son of **Uther** and **Igraine**, King Arthur grow up not as a noble to hid the result of the infidelity Uther committed. Though, with the scheme from **Merlin**, Arthur ended up on the throne again, enraged his half-sister **Morgan** because of that, and from then on have the responsibility of maintaining the **court of Camelot** and fighting against **the invaders.**
What's fascinating about King Arthur's story, of course, is **The Quest for Holy Grail**. What's fascinating about it? Well, it's because of the core of what Arthurian Legend actually is. ## What is Arthurian Legend? Arthurian Legend is not one story, in fact, it was built from very different authors retelling the story of the same king. The most famous one being **Le Morte d'Arthur** by **Thomas Malory**, which became the basis for the character **Artoria Pendragon** from **Fate** series by **Kinoko Nasu.**
Back to the topic, what exactly is Arthurian Legend? If I may say, Arthurian Legend is a series of tradition that evolve more and more following the progress of it's author's view about the world: from chivalric ideal to purity of heart. Those views are perhaps the reason why Arthurian Legend looks so messed up when you think about it. ## What's so Messed Up About It? To be continued.
